page: Amazon Web Services g7e.12xlarge is a graphics-intensive server instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features 48 vCPUs and 24 physical cores utilizing the x86_64 Intel Xeon 8559C processor running at 2.4 GHz. The CPU architecture includes a 320 MB L3 cache and supports Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512) and Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for accelerated matrix mathematics. The instance is equipped with 512.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 6 GHz, providing 10.67 GB of RAM per core.

This instance integrates dedicated hardware accelerators, specifically two NVIDIA Blackwell RTX Pro 6000 GPUs, delivering a combined 192 GB of VRAM. Local storage consists of a bundled 3800 GB NVMe SSD. Network connectivity is supported by a 400 Gbps baseline bandwidth, enabling high-throughput data transfers.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier performance in LLM inference tasks, with prompt processing speeds reaching 11238.84 tokens per second for a 135M model and 1264.93 tokens per second for a 70B model. Geekbench single-core execution and image processing also rank in the top 10% tier. Memory bandwidth and multi-core CPU performance fall within the top 25% tier, while standard stress-ng CPU benchmarks register in the middle 50% range.

Given the high density of DDR5 memory, dual Blackwell GPUs, and NVMe storage, this instance offers a specialized hardware profile for resource-intensive workloads. It is designed for large-scale LLM inference, graphics rendering, image processing, and database operations requiring high-speed local storage and substantial memory bandwidth.
description: Amazon Web Services g7e.12xlarge is a graphics-intensive server instance built on the Nitro hypervisor. It features 48 vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8559C processor, 512 GB of 6 GHz DDR5 memory, and a bundled 3800 GB NVMe SSD. Acceleration is driven by two NVIDIA Blackwell RTX Pro 6000 GPUs with 192 GB of total VRAM. Network performance is supported by a 400 Gbps baseline bandwidth. In benchmark testing, the instance demonstrates top-tier performance in LLM inference, image processing, and Geekbench single-core metrics, alongside strong memory bandwidth and database throughput. This hardware profile is optimized for large-scale language model deployment, graphics rendering, and data-intensive workloads requiring high-speed local storage.
og_description: Amazon Web Services g7e.12xlarge is a graphics-intensive server featuring 48 vCPUs, 512 GB DDR5 RAM, two NVIDIA Blackwell GPUs with 192 GB VRAM, and 3800 GB NVMe storage, delivering top-tier LLM inference performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services g7e.12xlarge features 48 vCPUs, 512 GB DDR5 RAM, two NVIDIA Blackwell GPUs, and a 3800 GB NVMe SSD for graphics and LLM workloads.
tagline: An accelerator-driven platform featuring dual GPUs, high-speed DDR5 memory, and NVMe storage for advanced machine learning and graphics workloads.
bullet_points: Two NVIDIA Blackwell RTX Pro 6000 GPUs with 192 GB total VRAM
48 vCPUs via Intel Xeon 8559C processor with AMX and AVX-512 support
512 GB of high-speed 6 GHz DDR5 memory
Bundled 3800 GB NVMe SSD local storage
Top-tier benchmark results for LLM inference and image processing
